pattern: Sort the array; the median position is fixed at n//2; elements above the median that are below k must be raised, and elements at or below the median that exceed k must be lowered.
hints: Sort nums. The median is at index m = n//2. To make the median equal to k, nums[m] must equal k after operations.

Elements at indices > m that are less than k pull the median down if swapped — but the median index is fixed after sorting. Focus: elements at index < m that are > k must be lowered to k; elements at index > m that are < k must be raised to k; nums[m] itself must move to k.

For each element at index i <= m: if nums[i] > k, add (nums[i] - k) to cost (elements at or below median that exceed k must shrink to k). For index i >= m: if nums[i] < k, add (k - nums[i]) to cost. Sum gives the answer.

edge_inputs.description: median already equals k, no operations needed
single element, just move it to k
two elements, median is larger one, bring it down
k larger than all elements, raise median index and upper half
even-length array median is the larger middle (index n//2)
elements below median already higher than k, need to be reduced

stress.description: random arrays to test cost computation correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    return [nums, k]
